bullets.text: The US military has killed Niño Guerrero, the leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang, in an airstrike, as confirmed by President Trump.

An investigation by Global Witness suggests that major global brands, including Amazon and Sony, may be using coltan, a mineral sourced from supply chains controlled by M23 rebels accused of atrocities in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
Kenyan police shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Nanyuki, approximately 120 miles from Nairobi.
Early childhood educators in Australia are experiencing burnout and stress due to increased workload, impacting their mental health and safety, with operators noting they are 'paid peanuts'.
An accounting firm, KPMG, is facing a significant scandal, leading to the resignation of several senior leaders and an ASIC investigation into its business practices.
A woman in her 30s was hospitalized with arm and leg injuries after a shark attack at Coogee beach, leading to the closure of several beaches in Sydney's east.
Wall Street orchestrated the largest IPO in history for SpaceX by convincing investors to back a futuristic strategy, despite significant losses, and grant full control to Elon Musk.
Artificial intelligence is fundamentally changing investment strategies, leading to a shift in how funds are allocated and risks are diversified across all asset classes.
Several global central banks, including the Fed and BOE, remain cautious regarding whether the Iran war poses a greater immediate threat to inflation or economic growth.
